summary: Soundboard‑derived recording (patched) sounds thin compared to the board, but the Fall ’85 East Coast show bursts with energy – standout Iko, Peggy‑O, Revolution and a fire‑stoked Other One.
review: The performance is praised for its high‑octane first set (Jerry’s soaring Iko, Peggy‑O and B.E. Woman) and a second half that delivers a smoking Revolution, Eyes of the World, The Other One → Miracle and an exuberant Sugar Magnolia encore. Reviewers note a few timing hiccups at the start of B.E. Woman and a lackluster Brent Mydland‑led song, while the patched soundboard transfer is described as sounding “way below” the board level.

ai_rating.rationale: Enthusiastic musical appraisal with many standout moments outweighs modest recording quality concerns; two reviews (one detailed) give a clear picture.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Patched transfer of Scott's soundboard source; reviewers say the audio sounds far below board level, indicating reduced clarity and presence.

poor_songs: Brent Mydland’s 'Baby Why You Want' (lacked umph)
show_quality.setlist_flow: First set described as a standard, high‑energy selection with gusto; second set builds momentum with fast‑paced jams and a strong encore, overall a “hot” show despite minor timing slips.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for soaring leads on Iko, Peggy-O and B.E. Woman, carrying the band through early timing lapses.
band_member_comments.Bob: Weir noted for bringing ‘the Rooster’ energy; vocal/guitar contributions highlighted positively.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent Mydland adds lyrical blush and twist, though his ‘Baby Why You Want’ felt under‑driven.
